content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation PPP of length NNN.

For each 1≤i≤N1\le i \le N1≤i≤N, find the number of subarrays of PPP with sum iii.

Note that a permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains a positive integer NNN −-− the length of PPP.
The second line contains NNN space separated distinct integers, P1,P2…PNP_1, P_2 \ldots P_NP1​,P2​…PN​ −-− denoting the permutation PPP.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, NNN space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the number of subarrays with sum iii.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only subarray is [1][1][1] with sum 111.
Test case 222:

For sum 111, the only possible subarray is [1][1][1].
For sum 222, the only possible subarray is [2][2][2].

Test case 333:

For sum 111, the only possible subarray is [1][1][1].
For sum 222, the only possible subarray is [2][2][2].
For sum 333, the possible subarrays are [3][3][3] and [1,2][1, 2][1,2].
